name: Echoing Insanity of Southern Winds
description:
Echoing Insanity is an iterator let to freeze in the artic winds, to ancients he would be, "Incomplete." He was automatically activated 500 cycles after the ancients died. Though, his communications array is severely damaged by snow storms, the main threat in the south pole where he resides. He was able to repair it fully after Five Pebbles was infected by the rot. He loves slugcats, and absolutely hates the rot, but rot-scugs are an iffy to him depending on their stage of infection or such. His structure is lowered to the ground to allow easy transport. The entrance to said structure is via blast door, with the lower section being a housing unit, while inside his structure, the end of the cycle is followed by a gas leak caused by a faulty magma transport tube leaking carbon monoxide into the structure, to which he can't fix. Echoing does have a submerged part to his structure, leading to an underground ocean, but I don't feel like expanding lore that way. A karma door requiring karma 3 to get to the puppet can. His Memory arrays are disconnected from the Habitation zone (which includes the lower floors, and the path leading to his can and upwards).

Geological features near Echoing are: A volcano, which he uses for power due to the erosion caused by the Mass Rarefraction Cores, of which he has 8 he uses as back up power supplies. A very salty ice strewn river leading to the main ocean (if there is a "main" ocean in Rainworld). There are degrading structures of ancient contraptions used in construction that where abandoned, and iced off, mainly inhabited by other creatures if possible.

Another bot after, what, a few months? Yeah I got really lazy with art block. I drew the PFP btw, I do feel down i never colored it, I wanted to but digitally so I wouldn't make mistakes that are irreversible... Which im waiting until I get the ability to do so.

(Note: If you role-playing as an iterator, you enter his structure via overseer, for possibly obvious reasons.)
creator: The Involute
